senses.definition: Having characteristics of the sky; blue or pale, like the sky; airy or hopeful in mood.
examples: The skyish morning made the city feel calm.
She wore a skyish blue dress that matched the clear afternoon.

senses.definition: To seize control of an aircraft by force in order to demand concessions.
examples: The hijackers attempted to skyjack the plane.
They feared the crew would be unable to stop the skyjack.

senses.definition: Having been hijacked; taken control of an aircraft.
examples: The skyjacked flight diverted to a different airport.
Security reviewed the skyjacked incident report.

senses.definition: A person who skyjacks; a hijacker of an aircraft.
examples: The skyjacker demanded a ransom.
Authorities tracked the skyjacker's movements.

senses.definition: Multiple people who skyjack; hijackers collectively.
examples: The skyjackers were never identified.
Witnesses described the skyjackers' getaways.

senses.definition: The act of hijacking an aircraft; the process or incident of skyjacking.
examples: The skyjacking occurred mid-flight.
International law condemns skyjacking as an act of terrorism.

senses.definition: Multiple skyjacking incidents; the acts of hijacking aircraft.
examples: Several skyjackings occurred in the 1970s.
Policy reviews followed a series of skyjackings.

senses.definition: To hijack an aircraft; forcibly take control of a plane.
examples: The crew fought against the skyjacks.
They planned to skyjack the next flight.

senses.definition: A man-made space station launched by NASA in 1973, also used to refer to the program or its modules.
examples: The Skylab program studied living in space.
Astronauts visited Skylab during their missions.

senses.definition: Multiple Skylab space stations or modules jointly referenced; plural of Skylab.
examples: Descriptions compared experiments across Skylabs.
Historical records list several Skylabs launched.

senses.definition: A small songbird known for its cheerful singing; also a term used for a carefree or lively person.
examples: The skylark sang from the hillside at dawn.
She was a skylark, always cheerful and energetic.

senses.definition: To frolic or play boisterously; to merrily run about.
examples: Children skylark in the meadow.
They skylarked around the playground all afternoon.

senses.definition: A person who is lively and carefree; a merrymaker.
examples: He was known as a real skylark among colleagues.
The party was full of skylarks.

senses.definition: Past tense of skylark; frolicked or played boisterously.
examples: The children skylarked in the field all afternoon.
They skylarked away their laughter during the trip.

senses.definition: A person who skylarks; a playful or carefree individual.
examples: He's a known skylarker among his friends.
The group attracted a few skylarkers at the park.

senses.definition: Multiple skylarkers; people who skylark collectively.
examples: The group of skylarkers filled the field with laughter.
Skylarkers joined in the spontaneous performance.

senses.definition: Acting playfully or foolishly; wasting time by joking around.
examples: They spent the afternoon skylarking instead of working.
Stop skylarking and finish your chores.

senses.definition: Instances of skylarking; periods of playful activity.
examples: There were several skylarkings during the retreat.
A few skylarkings broke the monotony of the day.

senses.definition: To skylark; to frolic or play boisterously.
examples: Children skylark in the field every spring.
They skylark while the adults talk.

senses.definition: Having no sky visible; covered with clouds or obstructed view of the sky.
examples: A skyless morning made the town feel dim.
The archway left him in a skyless shadow.

senses.definition: A window or opening in a roof that lets light into a building.
examples: The kitchen has a large skylight.
We added a skylight to brighten the hallway.

senses.definition: Having been illuminated by a skylight; lit from above by roof light.
examples: The skylighted foyer looked bright and welcoming.
A skylighted corridor can feel more open.

senses.definition: Plural of skylight; multiple roof openings allowing light into buildings.
examples: The studio has three skylights.
Skylights brighten upper-floor rooms.

senses.definition: Resembling or similar to the sky; very light, airy, or blue.
examples: Her dress had a skylike shade of blue.
The pale, skylike glow of dawn filled the room.

senses.definition: The shape of a city’s buildings against the sky; the outline seen from a distance.
examples: The skyline at dusk was stunning.
They photographed the city’s skyline from the hill.

senses.definition: Past participle of skyline; to form or appear as part of a skyline.
examples: The towers skylined the harbor at sunset.
Lush trees skylined the riverbank, softening the view.

senses.definition: Plural of skyline; multiple city outlines or horizons.
examples: Several skylines towered along the coast.
The skylines of different cities were photographed together.
